key_insight: k-means clustering on atomistic fingerprints can pick a small, information-rich subset of configurations that fits interatomic potentials more accurately than random sampling.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, lower-cost training of classical potentials from DFT data while maintaining or improving energy and force accuracy.
method: Build configuration fingerprints from CrystalNN descriptors and radial distribution functions (RDF).
Cluster the full dataset with k-means and select representative configurations from each cluster.
Fit an EAM potential to Ti using selected subsets and compare against random sampling; visualize similarity with t-SNE.
evidence_signal: Across subset sizes from 1800 configurations down to about 30, k-means gave consistently lower errors and standard deviations for both energies and forces than random selection.
watch_item: The method depends on the quality of the fingerprint representation, so overlapping regions in 2D visualization may hide subtle differences and should not be treated as proof of complete redundancy.
paper_title: k-Means Clustering in Fingerprint-Based Configuration Selection for Fitting Interatomic Potentials
